purpose: Force true mathematical attack before repo mutation, source shopping, physics matching, or candidate expansion. The protocol exists because full awareness degrades during long tool use; therefore major work must begin with a tool-light Stage 0 reasoning pass and only then select formal/computational tools.
core_rule: No major source-review, physical-comparator, route, substrate, or broad repo-expansion work may proceed before a Stage 0 math-first attack pass that states the object, exact finite/static targets, definitions, possible computations, falsifiers, and tool plan. Tools serve the math plan; tools do not choose the plan.

scope.primary_object: Formation Medium as pre-spacetime positive-geometric / time-geometric formation/readout structure
scope.initial_finite_range: n <= 7 or 8 when feasible; start smaller if definitions are unclear
scope.blocked_default_move: pattern-match to S-matrix, Ising machines, high-resonance, Route A, or CRBSM before formal/static targets are stated

stage_sequence.name: tool-light deep mathematical attack pass
required_questions: What is the actual mathematical object in this step?
Which definitions must be fixed before any computation?
What is the smallest literal finite object to compute?
What theorem, counterexample, or invariant would change the project?
What would show this direction is wrong or irrelevant?
Which existing theorem/source/check is a thin stick?
What should be deleted, demoted, or quarantined if this fails?
stage_sequence.required_output: A concise Stage 0 attack note or embedded critical_review_prework block that answers the questions before tool-heavy work begins.

stage_sequence.name: formal target extraction
stage_sequence.exit_condition: Exact statements exist for what must be computed or proved, including failure conditions and non-promotion boundaries.

stage_sequence.name: literal computations / finite checks / formal engines
allowed_work: Ax-Prover/Aristotle proof-search candidates validated only by Lean
stage_sequence.exit_condition: A concrete check result, counterexample, theorem skeleton, or failed attempt with artifacted assumptions.

stage_sequence.name: physics/engineering emergence after math
stage_sequence.exit_condition: Candidate physics/engineering is derived from or tested against Stage 0-2 math, not introduced by resemblance or convenience.

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: Stage 0 object definition, falsifier design, theorem/counterexample planning, deciding what not to do
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: claiming proof, source extraction, or final check status

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: small finite models, exact rational checks, graph/chord enumeration, residue-route combinatorics, artifact generation
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: pretending finite checks are all-n theorems

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: bounded predicate consistency, satisfiability/counterexample search, finite logical schemas
tool_escalation_matrix.current_status: available: Z3 CLI and Python reported ok
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: geometric theorem status without domain proof

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: formal theorem skeletons, locally validated proofs, typed definitions once statements stabilize
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: early vague exploration before definitions are stable

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: proof-search/formalization candidates only after Stage 1 statements exist
tool_escalation_matrix.current_status: configured but no submit tool exposed; output remains non-authoritative until Lean checks locally

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: candidate proof search against tracked Lean/theorem files when command is available
tool_escalation_matrix.current_status: permission enabled but command not present; output would remain candidate until Lean validates

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: polyhedral/combinatorial algebra, exact symbolic experiments, Grassmannian/positive-geometry finite computations
tool_escalation_matrix.current_status: available: SageMath 9.5 through D-backed WSL distro Ubuntu-22.04-D
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: blocking Stage 0 reasoning or pretending unavailable tools ran

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: polytope/face-lattice/canonical combinatorics and positive-geometry finite models
tool_escalation_matrix.current_status: available: polymake 4.11 through D-backed WSL distro Ubuntu-24.04-D
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: blocking smaller Python/Z3 finite checks when polymake is unavailable

tool_escalation_matrix.use_for: source verification, current tool/library status, external source candidates after Stage 0 states why source intake is needed
tool_escalation_matrix.do_not_use_for: pattern-matching physics before the math object and falsifier are stated

required_stage0_math_targets: minimal static object: Gr_+(0,n), Gr_+(1,n), n bounded explicitly
ordered residue route and sign/coherence definition
candidate state/bulk object and readout map
non-faithfulness condition: many bulk states mapping to same readout
settling relation before physics: admissible state relation, fixed point, minimizer, residue terminal, or other exact definition
first literal finite checks and what would falsify them
red_team_stop_conditions: work begins with source shopping or physical analogy instead of a mathematical object
tools are used to create motion before definitions are stable
a finite script is easier than asking the hard theorem/counterexample question
physics or engineering is selected before SG0-SG4 relevance is tested
new artifacts increase vocabulary without reducing uncertainty
the repo records a guard where an actual computation was needed
Stage 0 says nothing that could falsify, demote, or delete the direction

next_required_math_attack.question: What is the smallest exact formal skeleton of Formation Medium over Gr_+(0,n)/Gr_+(1,n), n <= 7 or 8, including readout, residue, bulk/readout map, non-faithfulness, and settling relation before any physics mapping?
next_required_math_attack.forbidden_shortcut: Do not continue SN-C3/Ising/high-resonance physical review before this Stage 0 skeleton exists or is explicitly deferred with reason.

non_promotion_rule: This protocol enforces math-first workflow only. It does not prove Formation Medium, define the final mathematical object, validate any physical regime, select a substrate, establish engineering readiness, or replace actual reasoning.
